items.displayName: Read data from the first sheet of InputTransactionData.xlsx into a DataTable
Rename the column 'Passengerid.' in the DataTable to 'Passengerid'
Voor elke rij in de datatabel

items.annotation:
Clean spaces for the variables FirstName, LastName, CityName, and StreetName.
Rules:
- Remove leading and trailing spaces.
- Ensure there is at most one space between words.
Keep activity titles short.

items.displayName: Remove leading/trailing spaces and reduce multiple internal spaces for the FirstName variable.
Remove leading/trailing spaces and reduce multiple internal spaces for the LastName variable.
Remove leading/trailing spaces and reduce multiple internal spaces for the CityName variable.
Remove leading/trailing spaces and reduce multiple internal spaces for the StreetName variable.

items.annotation: voeg iets toe wat "City raw: [" + CityName + "] | Street raw: [" + StreetName + "]" doet

items.displayName: Construct the string "City raw: [" + CityName + "] | Street raw: [" + StreetName + "]"

items.annotation:
Add two Assign activities in the current sequence.
1. Clean CityName
Set CityName = CityName.Replace(".", "").Replace("/", "").Replace("\"", "")
2. Clean StreetName
Set StreetName = StreetName.Replace(".", "").Replace("/", "").Replace("\"", "")
Keep titles short.
Do not create extra sequences.

items.displayName: Assign the cleaned value to CityName
Assign the cleaned value to StreetName
Log alle variabelen naar de console

items.annotation: valadting the process and logigng back to me

I already have the following variables in my UiPath workflow:

FlightDate (String)

FlightDateParsed (Date)

isFlightDateValid (Boolean)

Build a UiPath workflow using Assign, If, and Log Message activities that does the following:

Log the raw FlightDate string.

Use DateTime.TryParseExact to try converting FlightDate to the existing Date variable FlightDateParsed.

If parsing succeeds:

Log the parsed date (FlightDateParsed).

Check whether the date is tomorrow or later (FlightDateParsed.Date >= DateTime.Now.AddDays(1).Date).

Store the result in isFlightDateValid.

If parsing fails:

Log a warning message.

Set isFlightDateValid to false.

Please fill all expressions for Condition, Message, and Assign values and use the existing variable names.

I have the following variables in my UiPath workflow:

FlightDate (String)

FlightDateParsed (DateTime)

isFlightDateValid (Boolean)

Build a validation step using Assign, If, and Log Message activities that tries to parse FlightDate into FlightDateParsed using DateTime.TryParseExact with the format dd/MM/yyyy.

The workflow should:

Log the raw value of FlightDate.

Attempt to parse the value into FlightDateParsed.

If parsing succeeds, log the parsed date and set isFlightDateValid = true.

If parsing fails, log a warning message that includes the original FlightDate value and the expected format dd/MM/yyyy, and set isFlightDateValid = false.

Make sure all Condition, Assign, and Message expressions are filled and use the existing variables.

items.displayName: Attempt to parse the FlightDate string into a DateTime object named FlightDateParsed using the format dd/MM/yyyy.
Attempt to parse the FlightDate string into a Date variable (FlightDateParsed) using a specific format. The success of this parsing attempt will be stored in a boolean variable (isParsingSuccessful).
If the parsing was successful (isParsingSuccessful is True):

items.annotation:
Create an If activity to validate Age.
Step 1: Check if Age can be converted to an integer using Integer.TryParse.
Step 2: If the conversion succeeds, check if the parsed value is between 0 and 120.
Both 0 and 120 are valid values.
Step 3: If the value is within this range, set isAgeValid = True.
Otherwise set isAgeValid = False.
Step 4: If the Age cannot be converted to an integer, set isAgeValid = False.
